title: WorkflowProfile
description: A configurable state machine — the lifecycle as DATA. An ordered list of states (each just an id + display label) and the first-class transitions between them. Semantics are NOT tagged onto states: terminality is derived from the graph (a state with no outgoing transition is terminal), where work may be created is `entryStates`, and everything view-shaped (board columns, KPIs, overdue, SLA) is expressed downstream as rules over the recorded transition history. A WorkOrder.status is a state id; a WorkOrderType may name a profile (null = the org default). Applies equally to work requests and work orders — same machine, different profile. One JSON document = one profile.

key.description: Stable key referenced by WorkOrderType.workflowProfile, e.g. 'default'.

isDefault.description: True for the org's default profile, used when a type names no profile.

entryStates.description: State ids at which a record may be created / enter the machine. Requested-style work enters at the first state; schedule-generated work may enter mid-graph (e.g. 'planned'). Defaults to the first state if omitted. Creation is an action that targets one of these — it is not inferred from graph position.

states.description: The lifecycle states in display order. A state is just identity + label — no category, no initial/terminal flags (those are derived). Display order is presentation only.

transitions.description: The allowed moves between states — this set IS the allowable-transition configuration (an adjacency view groups them by `from`). A move not listed here is forbidden. Each transition carries how it fires (trigger), what must hold (guards) and what happens after (actions).

id.description: Stable state id, referenced by WorkOrder.status and by transitions. Rename the label freely; keep the id stable so history and rules don't break.

label.description: Verb shown on the action for a manual transition, e.g. 'Approve', 'Start work'.

guards.description: Boolean guard EXPRESSIONS that must all be true for the transition to be allowed (manual) or to fire (automatic). See the expression language below.

actions.description: Side effects run when the transition succeeds (notify, reserve/deduct parts, create follow-up, finalise costs, webhook, run automation).

trigger.description: What initiates the transition. Automatic triggers (onEntry / timer / condition) fire LAZILY — evaluated whenever the record is loaded or touched, matching the serverless deployment (no daemon). onEntry runs synchronously in the same operation; timers are measured from the recorded time the current state was entered. Default (omitted) = manual.

type.description: manual: a user initiates it (guards gate the button). onEntry: fires immediately on entering `from` (makes `from` a transient pass-through, e.g. cancelled → closed). timer: fires after a dwell in `from`. condition: fires when `when` becomes true.

afterHours.description: For type=timer: hours in the `from` state before firing.

afterDays.description: For type=timer: convenience alternative to afterHours (afterHours wins if both set).

when.description: For type=condition: a boolean expression; the transition fires when it evaluates true (and guards pass).

type.description: The side effect to run. Beyond this built-in set, 'run_automation' names a registered automation hook (config selects pre-written behaviour) and 'webhook' is the raw escape hatch.

target.description: Optional target — a role/team to notify, a webhook key, or an automation-rule id.

$comment: Guard / condition expression language: a small, safe boolean mini-language (no code execution). Grammar: literals (number, 'string', true, false, null), names (variables or 0/N-arg function calls), operators ! - * / + - < <= > >= == != && || and parentheses. Names resolve from an evaluation scope the host builds per record: history-derived helpers hoursInState([id]) / daysInState([id]) / inState(id); plus domain predicates and variables the host supplies (e.g. allTasksComplete(), partsReserved(), windowSet(), permitsIssued(), assigned(), role('approver'), withinApprovalLimit(), priority, cost). Examples: "daysInState('completed') >= 5", "allTasksComplete() && partsReserved()", "role('approver') && cost <= approverLimit".
